personal.name: Lovish Sharma
personal.title: Software engineer
personal.subtitle: Software engineer building scalable web platforms at Microchip, India.
bio: I'm Lovish — a Software Engineer based in Bangalore with 1.5+ years of professional experience building and shipping production-grade web applications. At Microchip Technology, I engineer the search and discovery infrastructure for microchip.com: I improved site-search relevancy by 22%, reduced null-result queries near to zero, and delivered internal admin tooling in Vue.js and .NET that product teams across the globe use daily.

My work spans frontend development (Vue.js, React.js, Next.js), backend API design (.NET Framework, Node.js, Express.js), and database engineering (SQL Server, PostgreSQL, MongoDB). I write code that is readable, testable, and built to scale — the kind of codebase the next engineer on the team is glad to inherit. Outside of work I ship personal projects: a Spotify-to-YouTube playlist converter using OAuth 2.0 and REST APIs, and a multi-tenant SaaS invoice platform with role-based access control and multi-country tax logic.

projects.title: Invixy
projects.description: A multi-tenant SaaS invoice management platform engineered with Next.js, Prisma ORM, and PostgreSQL. Features a dynamic tax-calculation engine supporting India (GST), Canada (HST/GST), and US tax regimes — eliminating manual tax computation for businesses. Authentication and authorization handled via NextAuth.js with role-based access control (RBAC) across three user personas: owner, accountant, and employee, each with scoped dashboards and permissions.

projects.title: SoundSwap
projects.description: A full-stack playlist migration platform that converts Spotify libraries to YouTube Music. Built with React.js on the frontend and an Express.js + Node.js backend connected to MongoDB. Implements Spotify and Google OAuth 2.0 for secure cross-platform authentication. The REST API handles track mapping and metadata retrieval at sub-300ms average response time, supporting real-time sync of playlists with 500+ tracks. Responsive Tailwind CSS UI with full mobile support.

projects.title: Daunku
projects.description: A performant frontend showcase for a furniture brand, built in React.js with Framer Motion for fluid animation choreography. Features a responsive product gallery, motion-driven page transitions, and layout compositions designed for conversion. Zero backend — pure frontend engineering with a focus on render performance and visual polish.

description: Lifted site-search relevancy by 22% by integrating Lucidworks Fusion, .NET APIs, and SQL Server, with a sharp drop in null-result queries.
Built and maintained admin applications in Vue.js, .NET, MongoDB, and SQL Server that streamline product-data management across categories.
Ideated and shipped TreeLink — a Vue.js + .NET app for creating and visualizing hierarchical product-category trees, improving catalog organization and discoverability.
Wrote PowerShell automation that replaced manual data-insertion processes across product systems.
Built a search-analytics dashboard surfacing query trends and zero-result rates as actionable insights.
Closed bugs across frontend and backend modules, tightening stability, usability, and load times.

now.building: TreeLink — hierarchical category trees in Vue.js + .NET
